title: Internal error on imported connection attempt
author: krwudtke
body_snippet:
<!--- Provide a general summary of the issue in the Title above -->
**Internal error on imported connection attempt**
We have an RD Session Host server farm with 2 servers and a gateway performing load balancing. We'll call the servers RDSSERVER03 & RDSSERVER04 and the gateway server GATEWAY01 with an alias of RDS2. There is an rds2.rdp file that many people in the company use to connect to the servers and it works just fine (see attached). I imported that file into mRemoteNG, but I can't get ...

iterations.description: An internal error during connection import indicates a defect in the deserialization or processing logic of imported connection data, requiring investigation and a fix.

comments.author: robertpopa22
comments.snippet:
**Fix available for testing**

A fix for this issue has been implemented in our community fork (`robertpopa22/mRemoteNG`, branch `main`). A beta build including this fix is available from the fork's Releases page.

Please test and report if this resolves your issue.

comments.author: krwudtke
comments.snippet:
I will but can you answer my questions here: **[CENSORED!] Links to external GitHub repositories are not allowed**
That link will probably get removed, but it is Issue 14 on your Community Edition.

notes:
AI triage (gemini): An internal error during connection import indicates a defect in the deserialization or processing logic of imported connection data, requiring investigation and a fix.
Approach: Investigate the XmlConnectionsDeserializer and MRemoteNGXmlImporter classes to identify the source of the internal error during connection import and implement a robust fix, potentially adding error handling or validation.
